name: research-notebook
label: Research Notebook
description: Research and exploration projects: Jupyter notebooks, data exploration, hypothesis testing, literature synthesis.
profile_description: Interactive exploration on local machine or remote compute. Reproducibility and evidence chains are first-class concerns.
run_context_additions: - State your hypothesis BEFORE running any analysis — record it in the first notebook cell or RESEARCH.md.
- Pin all library versions (requirements.txt or environment.yml) for reproducibility.
- Every figure must have a caption explaining what it shows and the data it was derived from.
- Track analysis lineage: what data, what transform, what parameters produced each result.
- Separate exploratory notebooks (scratch) from presentation notebooks (final) in directory layout.
workflow_notes: Formulate a falsifiable hypothesis before coding any analysis.
Use the `research-synthesis` skill to distill findings into a concise summary after each session.
Keep raw data immutable — never overwrite source data files; always derive transformed copies.
Record negative results and dead ends in PROGRESS.md — they are evidence too.
Restart the kernel and re-run all cells before treating any notebook as final.
safety_notes: Never include private datasets or PII in notebooks that may be committed to Git.
Add large data files (.csv, .parquet, .h5) to .gitignore before first commit.
Acknowledge statistical limitations (sample size, selection bias) in every conclusion.
Do not report preliminary results as confirmed findings — label all speculative conclusions clearly.
Backup intermediate results to a stable artifact store before destructive re-runs.
